channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: A US bomb from World War II explodes at a Japanese airport, causing a large crater in a taxiway
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — An unexploded U.S. bomb from World War II that had been buried at a Japanese airport exploded Wednesday, causing a large crater in a taxiway and the cancellation of more than 80 flights but no injuries, Japanese officials said.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Israel, Iran swap threats as strikes on Lebanon continue
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has vowed to retaliate, saying Iran “made a big mistake" by launching a missile attack.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Israel to have 'substantial response' to Iran attacks: John Bolton
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, less than a day after Israel began ground attacks on the militant group Hezbollah in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, The Hill

item.title: Israel's multilayered air-defense system passes another test in fending off Iranian missile strike
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel's multilayered air-defense system appears to have passed another test after fending off Iran's latest missile barrage. In Tuesday's night's strike, Iran fired over 180 missiles toward Israel.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 4 candidates to lead Britain's defeated Conservatives bash Labour, and each other
item.description: The four contenders to lead Britain’s Conservative Party have taken turns to argue why they have what it takes to lead the right-of-center party back from a catastrophic election defeat.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ukraine's military will pull out of Vuhledar in the east after 2 years of intense fighting
item.description: Ukraine's military officials say forces are withdrawing from the front-line town of Vuhledar, perched atop a tactically significant hill in eastern Ukraine, after more than two years of grinding battle.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Palestinians in West Bank risk crossing Israel's separation barrier to flee failing economy
item.description: YATTA, West Bank (AP) — At dawn in mid-May, Sayyed Ayyed and dozens of other unemployed Palestinian men gathered at the foot of the towering wall of concrete and barbed wire dividing the occupied West Bank from Israel. A smuggler was there with a ladder and ropes.
item.dc:creator: JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israeli military says regular infantry, armored units joining limited ground operation in southern Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli military said regular infantry and armored units were joining what it has called its limited and localized ground operations in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Claudia Sheinbaum sworn in as 1st female president of Mexico
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Claudia Sheinbaum was sworn in Tuesday as Mexico’s first female president, riding the enthusiasm over her predecessor’s social programs but also facing challenges that include stubbornly high levels of violence.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ and MARIANA MARTÍNEZ BARBA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Under fire and using old equipment, Lebanon's rescuers struggle to respond to Israeli offensive
item.description: The civil defense forces of one of the world’s most war-torn nations are shocked at the destruction underway in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Sarah El Deeb, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Palestinian officials say 51 killed in Israeli operation on southern Gaza
item.description: Israel launched ground operations and carried out airstrikes in a hard-hit city in southern Gaza overnight.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Thai police seek negligence charges for a school bus fire that killed 23
item.description: Thai police are investigating whether the school bus fire that killed 23 young students and teachers in suburban Bangkok was caused by negligence after filing initial charges against the driver.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China marks 75 years of Communist Party rule as economic challenges and security threats linger
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China is marking the 75th year of Communist Party rule as economic challenges and security threats linger over the massive state.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump warns of 'global catastrophe' following Iranian missile attack on Israel
item.description: Former President Trump on Tuesday bashed President Biden and Vice President Harris in the wake of an Iranian missile attack on Israel, arguing if Harris is elected in November, it would lead to mass chaos on the world stage.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: 6 people killed in Tel Aviv shooting attack
item.description: Six civilians were killed Tuesday in a shooting in Tel Aviv, local police said, as tensions between Israel and Iran-backed terrorist groups in the region have dramatically escalated in recent days and weeks.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: What Iran attacks mean for Americans in Israel: Vittert
item.description: NewsNation's Leland Vittert believes there will not be immediate panic for most of the more than 500,000 Americans living in Israel.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: The five smallest countries in the world cover less than 50 square miles combined
item.description: Vatican City, Monaco, Nauru, Tuvalu and San Marino make up the world's smallest countries. The biggest of the group, San Marino, is only 25 square miles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico's Claudia Sheinbaum sworn in as North America's first female head of state
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum was sworn in at a joint session of the country’s Congress on Tuesday, becoming North America’s first female head of state.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Israel under attack: 2 gunmen kill 6 civilians, injure others near Tel Aviv
item.description: A mass shooting by at least two individuals near Tel Aviv has killed and injured multiple people — an incident authorities believe is terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran fired dozens of missiles into Israel on Tuesday, prompting Israel to order residents to remain close to bomb shelters, while the U.S. warned of severe consequences if Iran launched a ballistic missile against Israel.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US Embassy in Israel warns employees to shelter in place amid threat of Iran missile attack
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Jerusalem is cautioning all government employees and their family members to shelter in place until further notice as Iran prepares to launch missiles at Israel. The warning came as U.S. officials said they expected Iran to attack Israel with missile and drone strikes on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Mysteries of universe revealed? Hardly. But CERN still fascinates, discovers on its 70th anniversary
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The research center that is home to the world’s largest particle accelerator is celebrating its 70th anniversary on Tuesday, with the physicists who run it aiming to unlock secrets about dark matter and other mysteries to promote science for peace in today's conflict-darkened world.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian national indicted in ransomware attacks in US
item.description: A Russian national is accused in a series of ransomware attacks in Texas and other parts of the U.S. over a seven-year period, the Department of Justice (DOJ) announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels are looking to gain from continuing conflict in the Middle East
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — In the days since Israel intensified its campaign against Hezbollah in Lebanon, including the strike that killed the militant group's leader Hassan Nasrallah, Yemen's Iran-backed Houthi rebels have been quick to show they are an important player in the complex conflicts convulsing the M
item.dc:creator: FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NATO's new chief makes Ukraine support a top priority and says he will work with any US president
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The new head of NATO vowed on Tuesday to help shore up Western support for war-ravaged Ukraine and expressed confidence that he can work with whoever is elected president of the United States, the alliance's most powerful member, in November.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Video shows Russian fighter jet flying within feet of US F-16 near Alaska
item.description: The release of the video comes after a series of Russian incursions into the Alaska Air Defense Identification Zone.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hezbollah says it will choose Nasrallah's successor 'at the earliest opportunity'
item.description: Hezbollah’s second-in-command, deputy leader Naim Qassem, says the terrorist group will choose its new leader "at the earliest opportunity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Who were the 7 high-ranking Hezbollah officials killed over the past week?
item.description: The move left Lebanon and much of the Mideast in shock as Israeli officials celebrated major military breakthroughs.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden says he will talk to Netanyahu as Israel pummels Sunni terror targets in Beirut
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces reportedly began striking the Lebanese capital of Beirut on Sunday evening after President Biden said he intended to speak with Prime Minister Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lithuanian FM warns Russia can do 'so much damage to its neighbors'
item.description: Lithuania has been one of the most vocal opponents of Russia since Moscow's invasion of Ukraine in 2022. The NATO country has raised its defense spending to over 3.2% annually.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli Air Force strikes Houthi targets in Yemen with 'extensive' operation
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces said the Israeli Air Force struck Houthi terror targets, including power plants and a seaport used to import oil, in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in hiding with extra security following Hezbollah leader's death: report
item.description: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was placed in a secure location after Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah's killing in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IDF announces death of another senior Hezbollah official following Nasrallah death
item.description: The Israeli military says it killed yet another member of Hezbollah's top command on Sunday, after wiping out leadership in a Friday strike.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: European officials dismiss claim world leaders 'are laughing' at Trump, praise his 'strong message'
item.description: Vice President Harris, during September's presidential debate, claimed world leaders were "laughing" at former President Trump. Several foreign officials beg to differ.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump and Zelenskyy meet in New York as election holds high stakes for US support for Ukraine
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met face-to-face with Donald Trump on Friday with public tensions rising between the two over Ukraine's defense against Russia's invasion and in the midst of the U.S. presidential election.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russian missiles and drones target Kyiv for 5 hours and hit Ukraine's power grid
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine’s air defenses battled an overnight Russian aerial attack on the capital Kyiv for five hours, officials said Thursday, as missiles and drones again hammered the Ukrainian power grid. The Kyiv attack injured at least two people, Ukraine’s Emergency Service said.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation
